data.executiveSummary: The order-worker service faced significant issues due to consumer group lag, leading to stalled partition rebalances. The issue resulted from a series of KafkaExceptions, indicating that the consumer group was falling behind, which might have impacted the processing of Kafka messages.

rootCause.title: Kafka Consumer Group Lag
rootCause.description: The root cause of the incident was a series of KafkaExceptions in the order-worker service, indicating that the Kafka consumer group was falling behind and the partition rebalance processes were stalled.

evidenceChain: Loki logs show repeated KafkaExceptions with the message 'Consumer group falling behind. Partition rebalance stalled processing.' in the order-worker service.

timeline.event: First KafkaException detected in logs indicating rebalance stall.
Continued KafkaExceptions indicating persistent rebalance stalls.

data.impact: Users experienced delays in order processing, as the order-worker service could not keep up with Kafka message consumption due to stalled partition rebalances.

similarIncident.title: Redis Connection Timeout Spikes
similarIncident.resolution: Resolution: Restarted redis-primary-3 instance due to orphaned connections.

recommendedActions: Investigate and resolve the cause of the Kafka consumer group lag to prevent rebalance stalls.

Consider applying a similar resolution strategy as the historically resolved incident (KAN-6) which involved restarting instances to resolve connection issues, potentially applicable if resource contention is found.
Monitor Kafka consumer lag metrics and set alerts for early detection of lag and rebalance issues.
